title: The Pulse: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ as a weird new trend - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: At Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce and other large companies, devs are purposefully burning tokens (and money!) to inflate their AI usage and hit AI usage metrics which they treat as targets.

title: The Pulse: is GitHub still best for AI-native development? - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: Availability has dropped to one nine (~90% – !!), partly due to not being able to handle increased traffic from AI coding agents. There’s also no CEO and an apparent lack of direction.

title: Is the FDE role becoming less desirable? - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: Job postings for Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) have surged, but many professionals don’t want the role because it’s more like solutions engineering than software development.

title: The Pulse: Cloudflare rewrites Next.js as AI rewrites commercial open source - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: An engineer at Cloudflare rewrote most of Vercel’s Next.js in one week with AI agents. It looks like a sign of how AI will disrupt existing moats and business models. Analysis

title: I replaced a $120/year micro-SaaS in 20 minutes with LLM-generated code - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: I used to pay $120/year for a SaaS that hasn’t added new features in four years, and didn’t fix its broken billing system for three years. Using an LLM, I managed to rewrite all the functionality I used to pay for in 20 minutes. Is this bad news for “write once, don’t update later” SaaS?

title: The grief when AI writes most of the code - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: When AI writes almost all code, what happens to software engineering? There is grief involved for us developers, that's for sure.

title: The Pulse: Cloudflare’s latest outage proves dangers of global configuration changes (again) - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: Deja vu: a large Cloudflare outage caused by an instantly rolled-out global config change – two weeks after a similar problem

title: The Pulse: Could a 5-day RTO be around the corner for Big Tech? - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: From next February, workers at Instagram must be in the office, five days a week. This makes Meta the second tech giant after Amazon to mandate a 5-day RTO. Will more big companies do the same?

title: Downdetector and the real cost of no upstream dependencies - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: During the Cloudflare outage, Downdetector was also unavailable. I got details from the team about why they have a hard dependency on Cloudflare, and why that won’t change anytime soon.

title: A startup in Mongolia translated my book - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: A 30-person startup called Nasha Tech translated The Software Engineer's Guidebook for the benefit of their company and the Mongolian tech ecosystem.

title: The Pulse: Cloudflare takes down half the internet – but shares a great postmortem - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: A database permissions change ended up knocking Cloudflare’s proxy offline. Pinpointing the root cause was tricky – but Cloudflare shared a detailed postmortem. Also: announcing The Pragmatic Summit

title: Four years on writing a tech book: pitching to a publisher - The Pragmatic Engineer
description:
In 2019, I decided to write a book about software engineering. As an experienced software engineer and manager, I had the topic clear in my head, and assumed the whole project would take between six and 12 months in writing and publishing it.

In the end, this process took several

title: The Pulse: Amazon layoffs – AI or economy to blame? - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: Amazon is doing more mass layoffs, claiming it wants to be more nimble. But are job losses really about US economic fears, and how Amazon’s retail business will be affected?

title: Comparing interviews at 8 large tech companies - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: Puneet Patwari applied to 8 major tech companies, and received 6 offers. He compares his interview experiences at Meta, Amazon, Uber, and 5 other workplaces

title: New trend: programming by kicking off parallel AI agents - The Pragmatic Engineer
description: More devs are experimenting with kicking off coding agents in parallel
